reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly counts integer pairs (x,y) with x² + xy + y² ≤ 2012. The bounding box Finset.Ico (-n) (n+1) × Finset.Ico (-n) (n+1) with n=2012 gives integer coordinates in [-2012, 2012]². Since 4(x² + xy + y²) = (2x+y)² + 3y², any solution has |y| ≤ √(8048/3) ≈ 51.8 and similarly |x| ≤ 51, so the bounding box comfortably contains every integer solution — no solutions are missed. The filter condition p.1² + p.1·p.2 + p.2² ≤ n (with n : ℕ coerced to ℤ) exactly matches the informal inequality in ℤ × ℤ. The conclusion ¬(3 ∣ countPoints 2012) faithfully encodes 'the number ... is not divisible by 3'. The unused quadraticFormPoints definition does not affect the theorem statement.
